import numpy as np

# Original 4x4 data matrix from the example
X = np.array([
    [2,1],
    [3,2],
    [4,3],
], dtype=float)

print("Original matrix X:")
print(X)









​Original matrix X:
[[2. 1.]
 [3. 2.]
 [4. 3.]]

# --------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Compute covariance matrix
# Each row is an observation, each column is a feature
# --------------------------------------------------
cov_matrix = np.cov(X_centered, rowvar=False)

print("\nCovariance matrix:")
print(cov_matrix)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Compute column means and center the data
# --------------------------------------------------
means = X.mean(axis=0)
X_centered = X - means

print("\nColumn means:")
print(means)

print("\nCentered matrix X_centered:")
print(X_centered)
​
Column means:
[3. 2.]

Centered matrix X_centered:
[[-1. -1.]
 [ 0.  0.]
 [ 1.  1.]]

# --------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Eigen decomposition of covariance matrix
# eigh is used because covariance matrices are symmetric
# --------------------------------------------------
eigenvalues, eigenvectors = np.linalg.eigh(cov_matrix)

print("\nUnsorted eigenvalues:")
print(eigenvalues)

print("\nUnsorted eigenvectors (columns):")
print(eigenvectors)
​
Unsorted eigenvalues:
[0. 2.]

Unsorted eigenvectors (columns):
[[-0.70710678  0.70710678]
 [ 0.70710678  0.70710678]]

# --------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Project data onto principal components
# Full 4D projection
# --------------------------------------------------
Z_full = X_centered @ eigenvectors

print("\nProjected data onto all 4 principal components:")
print(Z_full)

​
Projected data onto all 4 principal components:
[[-1.41421356  0.        ]
 [ 0.          0.        ]
 [ 1.41421356  0.        ]]

# --------------------------------------------------
# Optional: Reduce to first 2 principal components
# --------------------------------------------------
k = 2
W2 = eigenvectors[:, :k]
Z2 = X_centered @ W2

print(f"\nProjection onto first {k} principal components:")
print(Z2)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Sort eigenvalues/eigenvectors in descending order
# --------------------------------------------------
idx = np.argsort(eigenvalues)[::-1]
eigenvalues = eigenvalues[idx]
eigenvectors = eigenvectors[:, idx]

print("\nSorted eigenvalues (largest first):")
print(eigenvalues)

print("\nSorted eigenvectors (columns = principal components):")
print(eigenvectors)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Explained variance ratio
# --------------------------------------------------
explained_variance_ratio = eigenvalues / eigenvalues.sum()

print("\nExplained variance ratio:")
print(explained_variance_ratio)
​
Sorted eigenvalues (largest first):
[2. 0.]

Sorted eigenvectors (columns = principal components):
[[ 0.70710678 -0.70710678]
 [ 0.70710678  0.70710678]]

Explained variance ratio:
[1. 0.]
